mission.title: Install and Configure Bootc on Kubernetes
mission.description: Bootc is a tool for transactional, in-place operating system updates using OCI/Docker container images. Installing Bootc allows you to manage and upgrade your operating system seamlessly using container images.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bootc Helm repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add bootc https://charts.boo.tc
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Bootc using Helm
steps.description:
Install Bootc with the following command, creating a namespace if it doesn't exist:
```bash
helm install bootc bootc/netbox --namespace bootc --create-namespace --version 4.1.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Bootc pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bootc
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Bootc deployment:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment bootc --namespace bootc --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Bootc release with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall bootc --namespace bootc
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Bootc:
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Bootc namespace to clean up all associated resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace bootc
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace bootc -o yaml > bootc-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to get the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Bootc
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Bootc to the latest version with the following command:
```bash
helm upgrade bootc bootc/netbox --namespace bootc --version 4.1.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Bootc pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bootc
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the Bootc pods are stuck in Pending state, check for resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace bootc
``` 
Ensure there are enough resources available in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Bootc pods are in CrashLoopBackOff, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace bootc
``` 
Investigate any errors in the logs and adjust configurations as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating the Helm release is not found, ensure that the release name is correct and the namespace is specified:
```bash
helm list --namespace bootc
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Bootc will have all pods running in the 'bootc' namespace, and you will be able to manage your operating system updates using container images. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bootc https://charts.boo.tc
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment bootc --namespace bootc --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl.
